title: Maine man missing since early April found dead, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News Twelve days after the man was last seen, his body was found on a dirt road in the town where he went missing, according to police.
Maine State Police are investigating the death of a Thomaston man after he went missing earlier this month, the agency said.
Darryl Sanborn, 64, was last seen in Waldoboro, Maine on April 5, State Police said in a press release.

title: Boston police looking for suspects after 2 bank armed robberies Tuesday morning
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime A Santander Bank and a TD Bank were both robbed nearly two hours apart, Boston police said.
The two suspects from a robbery at a Santander Bank in Roxbury.
Boston Police Department Boston police are searching for suspects involved in two Tuesday morning bank robberies that used the same getaway car, police said.

title: Terry Francona reacts to Alex Cora being fired by Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "It does happen, unfortunately, and it’s part of the job, and you kind of know that going in.
” Alex Cora and Terry Francona met on the baseball diamond in March.
AP Photo/Carolyn Kaster Terry Francona is no stranger to the pressures of the Boston market.

title: After 74 years, Dairy Witch Ice Cream in Salem will scoop no more
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Food News The beloved shop was opened in 1952 by Beatrice and Peter Polemenako before their daughter carried on the business.
After 74 years in business, Dairy Witch Ice Cream in Salem is closing its doors, the shop announced Saturday.
“We will not be opening this spring,” the business said on Facebook.

title: Mass. woman gets jail after unleashing swarm of bees during an eviction
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Local News “Hey, hey, hey, she has a truck full of bees,” one deputy can be heard telling another, who responded, “What?

” In an image from the Hampden County Sheriff’s Department, a sheriff’s deputy tries to wrestle a tower of beehives away from Rebecca Woods as the deputies sought to serve an eviction notice in Longmeadow, Mass.
, Oct.

title: Patriots reportedly exercise Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option for 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots Exercising a fifth-year option keeps Gonzalez under contract in New England through the 2027 season.
Christian Gonzalez is set for a nice pay raise in 2027.

title: Viktor Arvidsson ruled out of Bruins’ Game 5 vs. Buffalo due to injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "Our life is on the line.
We’ve just got to go out there and try to die on the ice.
” Viktor Arvidsson scored two goals in the first four games of this first-round series.

title: Dave Dombrowski admits Phillies tried to hire Alex Cora shortly after Red Sox firing
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "I had told him I had really come to the conclusion that if he took it, I was going to make a change.
" Alex Cora and Dave Dombrowski worked together in Boston for parts of two seasons.

(Jim Davis/Globe Staff) By Conor Ryan April 28, 2026 | 6:26 PM 2 minutes to read It didn’t take long for several MLB writers and insiders to connect the dots between Alex Cora and the Philadelphia Phillies.

title: Somerville has been trying to fire a police detective since 2018. He’s still getting paid.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News After prosecutors said the detective will never be called to testify in court because of alleged misconduct, Dante DiFronzo was placed on paid leave in 2021 due to an incident years earlier.
The Somerville City Hall in Somerville.

Kayla Bartkowski For The Boston Globe A Somerville police detective at the center of a nearly decade-long battle between the police union, prosecutors, and the city has earned more than a half million dollars in leave pay as the city fights to keep him off the force.
